product.title: Buu / Kanban Status PWA
product.subtitle: Canonical Plan V2 sprint board for serious autonomous work

currentSprint.title: Live status PWA replacement sprint
currentSprint.summary: This sprint replaces the stale status cockpit with a serious Buu/Kanban dashboard, removes the unsafe hardcoded Cloudflare token fallback, and leaves the repo ready for security audit, QA, and deployment handoff.
currentSprint.statusLine: Frontend implementation underway. Deployment remains gated to the deployment-worker lane.
checkpoints: Replace stale Akumetsu status content with Buu/Kanban status UI.
Keep the app useful on phone and desktop.
Return safe status JSON from /api/status with no hardcoded secret fallback.
Hand off to security audit, QA, deployment, live QA, reviewer, and Buu closeout.

roadmap.title: Kanban Dashboard Serious Sprint
roadmap.label: Active now
roadmap.summary: Prove the dashboard, dispatch flow, blockers, and closeout stay visible and clean during a real sprint.

roadmap.title: Web / PWA App Sprint
roadmap.label: Active now
roadmap.summary: Ship a real visible web/PWA slice with responsive UI, local validation, and reviewable evidence.

roadmap.title: Backend / API / Data Sprint
roadmap.label: Next recommended
roadmap.summary: Build a small local API/data contract with validation and clean frontend handoff after this live sprint closes.

roadmap.title: External Provider / Auth Sprint
roadmap.label: Foundation retained
roadmap.summary: Only use real provider credentials when a real feature needs them. No trophy metadata reads.

roadmap.title: Cron / Scheduled Workflow Sprint
roadmap.label: Queued
roadmap.summary: Test scheduled jobs, watchdog behavior, and failure alerts after the current implementation tracks settle.

roadmap.title: Desktop / PC Software Sprint
roadmap.label: Backlog
roadmap.summary: Build and package a small local desktop or CLI utility only after web and workflow tracks are proven.

roadmap.title: Android APK Sprint
roadmap.label: Queued later
roadmap.summary: Produce a real debug APK when Android-specific delivery becomes the goal. PWA installability does not count as APK testing.

roadmap.title: Controlled External Mutation Sprint
roadmap.label: Approval gate
roadmap.summary: Power tools and external mutations belong here only with exact target, rollback, and verification scope.

roadmap.title: Long-running Autonomous Workflow Sprint
roadmap.label: Later stage
roadmap.summary: Run a multi-card workflow over hours or days without micromanagement, while preserving status cadence and auditability.

roadmap.title: Farm Management App Autonomous Rebuild
roadmap.label: Long-term target
roadmap.summary: The endgame is a Buu-built production-ready rebuild under explicit approval, not a momentum-based takeover.

retainedEvidence.summary: R4-K proved one narrow, secret-aware, read-only Cloudflare DNS connector lane for dns-worker only. It remains valid foundation evidence, not a blanket permission to explore providers.
confirmed: Exact target-specific Cloudflare read-only metadata work succeeded.
Token handling stayed local and non-retained.
Reviewer kept the verdict at RETAIN WITH CAUTIONS.
cautions: Do not generalize R4-K into broad provider discovery.
Mutation still requires a separate later gate.
Trust earned was narrow and role-specific to dns-worker.
Protected-state inspection remains out of bounds unless separately approved.

nextGate.title: Backend / API / Data Sprint
nextGate.summary: Once the live status PWA sprint is closed, the next clean capability gain is a small local API/data sprint with explicit contract, validation, and QA.
reasons: It extends the web sprint into clean frontend/backend contracts.
It keeps provider work gated until a real feature needs it.
It strengthens the handoff chain before later mutation or long-running autonomy.

operatingRules: Per-unique-blocker policy: up to three scoped resolution attempts for each blocker.
No Farm App work, Aku/OpenClaw edits, DNS mutation, database work, or unrelated deployments in this sprint.
Secrets stay out of code, artifacts, diffs, and live responses.
Implementation, security, QA, deployment, reviewer, and closeout each keep their own lane.
